nodes.goal: Load Niterói OSM surface, parks, water, and roads from the local PBF in EPSG:4326 and emit the layer array downstream.

nodes.content:
import base64
import requests
import geopandas as gpd
from shapely.geometry import Point

# Fetch the Niterói land surface temperature raster (24 yearly bands,
# 2001–2024) and embed the bytes as base64 inside a single-row
# GeoDataFrame so the downstream join can reuse them without refetching.
url = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/urban-toolkit/autark/main/usecases/public/data/niteroi_lst_verao_2001_2024.tif'
resp = requests.get(url, timeout=120)
resp.raise_for_status()
geotiff_b64 = base64.b64encode(resp.content).decode('ascii')

gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    {'geotiff_b64': [geotiff_b64], 'band_count': [24]},
    geometry=[Point(0, 0)],
    crs='EPSG:4326',
)
return gdf
nodes.goal: Fetch the 24-band LST GeoTIFF (2001–2024) for Niterói and carry its bytes downstream as base64.
Bundle the OSM layers and the raster bytes row into a single 2-element input for the spatial join.
Re-ingest the OSM layers, load the LST raster, then spatially join LST band averages to roads within 1 km.
Per-road OLS regression of the 24-year LST series on the GPU — output warming angle and intercept.
Fan augmented roads (plus context layers) out to map and plot; route brush selections on roads back from plot to map.
Render the Niterói layer stack in 3D with roads coloured by warming angle.
Brushable scatter of warming angle vs LST baseline; brushing routes through the pool to highlight roads on the 3D map.
